rulings.Case Title: ELIZABETH DIANE SCHENK VS. ELIZABETH JANE MCCARTHY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Good Faith Settlement Determination
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 10, 2024 line 1. DEFENDANT MARY CASTELLANOS, SOTHEBY'S INTERNATIONAL REALTY, INC. NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT DETERMINATION is GRANTED. No opposition filed. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JAMES BONG ENTERPRISE, INC. VS. MARY BONG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) PES-19-303149 WITH CASE CUD-23-673620
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 10, 2024 line 5. DEFENDANT MARY BONG, CHARLES CHUA, CHARLESTON CHUA, KINGSTON CHUA AMENDED MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) PES-19-303149 WITH CASE CUD-23-673620 is DENIED. There is no dispute as to the title to the property. Moving party fails to cite any authority and provides no facts for the proposition that a probate matter, if resolved in the moving parties' favor, will result in the corporate Plaintiff not being able to prosecute the unlawful detainer proceeding. See Martin-Bragg v. Moore (2013) 219 Cal.App.4th 367. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SONIA MALO VS. THEODORE PARODI ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 10, 2024 line 7. DEFENDANT TEODORO PARODI SUED AS "THEODORE PARODI" DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. rulings.Case Title: SAM PENG VS. INGE MCCUNE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 10, 2024 line 8. DEFENDANT KENNETH MCCUNE, TIERRA MORRIS DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Defendant fails to cite any authority for the proposition that Plaintiff has to allege compliance with the additional relocation payment requirement without condition precedent. Defendant points to no part of the Complaint of judicially noticeable material re: "landlord's receipt of written notice form the Eligible Tenant of entitlement to the relocation payment along with supporting evidence." SFRO 37.9C(e)(2). Plaintiff alleged compliance with SFRO. =(501/SKF) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: 2425 24TH ST, LLC VS. JANICE BUTTERWORTH ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 10, 2024 line 12. DEFENDANT JANICE BUTTERWORTH, CARA GIVENS DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint sufficiently states an unlawful detainer cause of action based on nuisance. Complaint sufficiently states compliance with service of 10 Day Notice, assuming that such compliance was even required under SF Apartment Association v. CCSF (2024) 104 Cal.App.5th 1218. Defendant fails to point to any part of the complaint alleging that the operative notice terminating the tenancy was given "pursuant to  section [1946.1]" triggering compliance with CC 1946.1(h). =(501/SKF) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
